description: Glue boundary checks for convert mode -- testing how skills behave when adding to an existing project

boundaries.check: Vision refinement approval triggers persona addition for the new capability
boundaries.convert_difference: In S1, vision creation automatically triggers full persona discovery. In S3, approved vision changes trigger targeted persona addition. The output is incremental (1 persona) rather than exhaustive (all personas).
boundaries.evidence: vision-refine-proposal.md approved -> P2-team-collaborator.md created referencing approval

boundaries.check: New persona (P2) is correctly identified as the consumer of the new feature in the feature ship brief
boundaries.convert_difference: In S1, personas inform the initial feature inventory. In S3, the new persona validates that the new feature has a real consumer. The persona-feature link is checked rather than created.

boundaries.check: Feature ship brief's cross-reference table is consumed by writing-spec to produce correct dependency declarations
boundaries.convert_difference: In S1, writing-spec has no existing ACs to reference. In S3, the ship brief's impact table (6 existing ACs checked) feeds directly into the spec's System Dependencies section, which splits into Existing and New tables.
boundaries.evidence: feature-realtime-collab.md System Dependencies has 2 existing + 2 new dependencies matching ship brief

boundaries.check: New spec's cross-AC references (COL-1.4 -> AC-1.3, COL-4.3 -> AC-1.2) are validated by spec-code-sync
boundaries.s1_equivalent: Simulated sync (no code to check against)
boundaries.convert_difference: In S1, spec-code-sync could only check self-consistency. In S3, it validates cross-feature AC references and detects DRIFT-001 (overdue-checker needs update). This is the most significant convert-mode difference.
boundaries.evidence: spec-code-sync-report.md validates COL -> AC references and finds DRIFT-001

boundaries.check: Sync report's drift findings inform journey design -- J02 accounts for the overdue-checker WS extension
boundaries.convert_difference: In S1, journeys are created from specs alone. In S3, the sync report's drift findings (overdue-checker extension) are reflected in J02's Scenario 2 (system-initiated overdue broadcast). The journey design is drift-aware.
boundaries.evidence: J02 Scenario 2 exercises COL-3.1/3.2 which address DRIFT-001

boundaries.check: New journey J02 correctly declares J01 as prerequisite and does not contradict J01's assumptions
boundaries.s1_equivalent: No equivalent -- bootstrap creates all journeys simultaneously with no ordering
boundaries.convert_difference: This is a convert-mode-only boundary. J02 must be aware of J01's single-user assumptions and validate that multi-user scenarios do not break them. The conflict analysis table (5 assumptions checked, 0 conflicts) is convert-mode-only output.
boundaries.evidence: J02 Preconditions list J01 as prerequisite; Conflict Analysis table shows 5 checks, 0 conflicts

boundaries.check: No downstream artifact is produced before the vision refinement is approved
boundaries.s1_equivalent: No equivalent -- bootstrap mode has no approval gates
boundaries.convert_difference: The Approval Gate is a convert-mode-only checkpoint that blocks the pipeline until the user confirms proposed vision changes. In autopilot mode, the approval is simulated. This is a critical safeguard when modifying established project direction.
boundaries.evidence: vision-refine-proposal.md shows PENDING -> APPROVED transition before downstream artifacts proceed

boundaries.check: DRIFT-001 identifies that enabler-overdue-checker needs an Implementation Notes update
boundaries.s1_equivalent: No equivalent -- no drift possible in bootstrap
boundaries.convert_difference: This is a convert-mode-only boundary. When spec-code-sync detects drift, it creates a remediation action that feeds back into the spec-writing phase. The existing enabler-overdue-checker spec would need to be updated to reflect the new WS dependency.
boundaries.evidence: spec-code-sync-report.md DRIFT-001 identifies required update to enabler-overdue-checker
boundaries.note: Remediation not yet applied -- enabler-overdue-checker.md in the source project is unchanged. This is expected in a read-only analysis scenario.

summary.key_finding: Convert mode introduces 3 boundaries that do not exist in bootstrap mode: Approval Gate, DRIFT remediation feedback loop, and journey prerequisite chaining. All convert-mode-retested boundaries (5/5) show measurably different behavior from their S1 equivalents.
